metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `[ "$(rg -c 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md)" = 0 ]` with `! rg -q 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md` which reliably exits 0 iff no matches and is version-stable.
evidence: ripgrep exits with code 1 and may emit no output on zero matches (behavior is version-dependent)
Shell substitution of an empty string vs literal '0' produces a string-inequality, flipping the test result
VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT TAXONOMY type 1/6: structured vs prose assertion target and potential substring/anchor issue
findings.finding: AC1's proving command uses `rg -c` to assert zero forbidden-term matches, but when ripgrep finds no matches it may output nothing (empty string) rather than '0', causing `[ "$(rg -c ...)" = 0 ]` to silently fail even on a clean file (a false-negative). The reliable idiom is `! rg 'pattern' file`.
scenarios: On a CI runner with a ripgrep version that emits nothing for zero matches, `$(rg -c ...)` is empty-string, `[ '' = 0 ]` is false, and AC1 reports FAIL even though the template is clean
findings.suggested_fix: Proving command becomes: `test -f templates/AGENTS.md && ! rg -q 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md`

findings.finding: The proving command `rg -c 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md` == 0 is a verify-command defect: ripgrep exits with code 1 (not code 0 printing '0') when no lines match, so the shell comparison `== 0` against exit code or output is undefined/unreliable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 Proving line: '`rg -c 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md` == 0'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the proving line to: `! rg 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md` (exits 0 when no forbidden terms are found, exits 1 when any are present).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for the self-check, e.g.: `cd /tmp && cp templates/AGENTS.md AGENTS_perturbed.md && sed -i 's/BEGIN rebar-usage/BEGIN rebar-usage PERTURBED/' AGENTS_perturbed.md && python -m pytest tests/unit/test_client_template_sync.py --template=AGENTS_perturbed.md 2>&1 | grep -q FAILED`; or at minimum specify that the test contains a `_divergence_self_check` sub-test (mirroring the existing `_identical` pattern) whose failure is observable in CI output.
evidence: 'perturbing the delimited block makes the test fail' is behavioral prose, not a command
ANTI-FP note: `make test` is a universal command that does not specifically prove the self-check wiring
findings.finding: AC3's self-check ('perturbing the delimited block makes the test fail') is described in prose with no concrete command, so an executor has no deterministic in-session step to prove the failure path is wired correctly.
findings.location: AC3: 'Proving: `make test`; perturbing the delimited block makes the test fail.'
scenarios: Executor writes a test that passes unconditionally (e.g. skips comparison), `make test` stays green, AC3 is signed off, and the drift-guard provides no actual protection
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'The test module MUST include a `test_divergence_self_check` function (mirroring `test_skill_shared_sync.py`'s pattern) that deliberately corrupts the in-memory block and asserts the comparison fails; this self-check running under `make test` is the concrete proof.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the canonical-source boundary: specify whether the delimited rebar-usage region maps to the entire `docs/ticket-model.md` file (enabling whole-file byte comparison) or to a named sub-section/multi-file set — and if the latter, specify the symmetric extraction step the drift-guard test must apply to the source side.
evidence: Scope: 'the canonical portable rebar-usage content lands in docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop) first'
Design decisions: 'the test asserts that block is byte-identical to the canonical source (mirroring _identical + a divergence self-check)'
The existing _identical() in test_skill_shared_sync.py reads whole-file bytes; a multi-file or sub-section canonical source breaks this pattern without a parallel extraction step
docs/ticket-model.md does not yet exist (confirmed absent in repo listing and ticket signature 'dep absent templates/AGENTS.md')
The dependent ticket 073d-51d8-3e58-4b14 (S1) which creates docs/ticket-model.md is open with no merged signature
findings.finding: The plan asserts the drift-guard test will compare the delimited block 'byte-identical to the canonical source,' but the canonical source is never pinned to a single, complete file. The Scope section says it 'lands in docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop) first' — the '+ the workflow loop' clause implies the rebar-usage content may span more than one source file or section. If the canonical source is a sub-section of docs/ticket-model.md rather than the whole file, the existing _identical() pattern (whole-file read_bytes()) cannot work and the test mechanism needs a symmetric delimited extraction on the source side — which the plan never specifies.
findings.location: Scope section: 'canonical portable rebar-usage content lands in docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop) first'; Design decisions: 'the test asserts that block is byte-identical to the canonical source'
scenarios: Implementer opens docs/ticket-model.md (created by S1) and finds it contains 4 concept sections + cross-refs to event-schema.md; the 'workflow loop' content is elsewhere. The delimited block in templates/AGENTS.md cannot be byte-identical to the entire ticket-model.md file — the test would always fail.

Implementer chooses to embed only the ticket-model.md content in the delimited block (omitting the workflow loop), making the block not byte-identical to any single canonical source that includes both pieces.
findings.suggested_fix: Pick one of: (a) dedicate a standalone `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` file whose full content is byte-identical to the delimited block, so the whole-file _identical() pattern works directly; or (b) if the block embeds only part of docs/ticket-model.md, add a BEGIN/END delimiter pair to that source file too and spec the test to extract the matching region from both sides before comparing.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve 'docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop)' to a single explicit canonical source file path (or, if two files are concatenated, specify both paths and the concatenation order) so the test implementation is unambiguous.
evidence: 'docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop)' — the parenthetical is unexplained
AC line: 'the test asserts that block is byte-identical to the canonical source' — canonical source is not a single unambiguous file path
ABSENCE: no file path is given for 'the workflow loop' content
findings.finding: The canonical source for the delimited region is described as 'docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop)' — the '+ the workflow loop' clause is unresolved: is the canonical source one file or two, and if two, which file contains the workflow loop content? The drift-guard test must know the exact file path(s) to open and compare against, so this ambiguity directly blocks correct test implementation.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the canonical portable rebar-usage content lands in `docs/ticket-model.md` (+ the workflow loop) first'
scenarios: Executor opens only docs/ticket-model.md for the comparison, missing the workflow-loop portion, producing a test that silently under-covers the delimited region
Executor must stop to ask: is the canonical source one file or two?
findings.suggested_fix: State the canonical source as a single file, e.g. 'docs/ticket-model.md § Rebar Usage (workflow loop section is included in that file)' — or if it is two files, write: 'canonical source = docs/ticket-model.md + docs/workflow-loop.md, concatenated in that order'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the region-extractor must assert (not silently return empty) when either the BEGIN or END marker is missing or when the extracted region is zero bytes, so marker absence is itself a test failure.
evidence: Plan text: 'the test asserts that block is byte-identical to the canonical source (mirroring _identical + a divergence self-check).'

test_skill_shared_sync.py's _identical() compares whole files — it cannot return a false-pass on a missing file. The new region-extractor design introduces a new failure mode not present in the model it mirrors.
AC3 self-test only covers 'perturbing the delimited block makes the test fail' — it does not cover the case where the delimiters themselves are absent.
No plan text specifies what the extractor returns or how the test asserts when markers are missing.
findings.finding: The drift-guard test's region-extraction logic has no specified fallback when BEGIN/END markers are absent or malformed in the template. A naive extractor returning empty bytes would cause the test to silently pass when the generated block is deleted — the exact failure mode the test is meant to catch.
findings.location: Design decisions section: 'the test asserts that block is byte-identical to the canonical source'; AC3: 'A drift-guard test compares ONLY the delimited region against the canonical source, fails on divergence'
scenarios: A developer accidentally deletes the BEGIN/END block while editing templates/AGENTS.md; the region extractor returns b'' which compares equal to b'' extracted from a non-existent or empty canonical, so the drift gate stays green.
A merge conflict corrupts the BEGIN marker to 'BEGIN rebar-usage-CONFLICT'; the extractor silently returns empty and the test passes.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design decisions section: 'The region-extractor raises AssertionError (or the test asserts the extracted length > 0) if either delimiter is absent, treating a missing block as a drift failure, not a match.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief note to `CLAUDE.md` (or a lightweight ADR) documenting the delimited-region sync pattern — what the BEGIN/END markers mean, that the enclosed block is generated/not-hand-edited, and how to re-sync — so the maintenance contract is discoverable without reading the test.
evidence: 'Keep the canonical portable rebar-usage prose in one source; embed it in templates/AGENTS.md between <!-- BEGIN rebar-usage (generated; do not edit) --> / <!-- END rebar-usage -->'
'the test asserts that block is byte-identical to the canonical source'
No mention of CLAUDE.md, ADR, or design-doc update anywhere in the plan
The inline 'Design decisions' section is part of the ticket, not a committed repository artifact
findings.finding: The delimited-region sync pattern is a novel maintenance contract (template ↔ canonical doc, enforced by BEGIN/END markers and a byte-identical assertion) but no update to CLAUDE.md, an ADR, or a persistent design doc is planned to capture this convention for future maintainers.
findings.location: Design decisions section; AC line 3 (drift-guard test); Scope section
scenarios: A future contributor edits the canonical source region in docs/ticket-model.md without knowing they must re-sync the template, gets a confusing test failure with no doc to explain the convention.
A new team member looks for 'how do we keep templates in sync with docs?' and finds nothing in CLAUDE.md or an ADR — the pattern is undiscoverable without reading test source.
findings.suggested_fix: In CLAUDE.md, under a 'Template sync' or 'Generated content' section, add 2–4 lines: the BEGIN/END marker convention, the canonical source path, and the instruction to run `make test` after editing the source. Alternatively, a one-paragraph ADR (e.g., `docs/adr/template-sync.md`) suffices.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and document the UPDATE workflow for the embedded delimited region (e.g., a `make sync-template` target or a documented copy command) so that when `docs/ticket-model.md` changes, the remediation path is unambiguous.
evidence: 'Keep the canonical portable rebar-usage prose in one source; embed it in templates/AGENTS.md between ... the test asserts that block is byte-identical to the canonical source'
ABSENCE: no 'make sync-template', no helper script, no documented copy step is mentioned anywhere in the plan for the UPDATE path when the canonical source changes legitimately
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the drift-guard test DETECTS when the delimited region diverges from its canonical source, but never specifies HOW a developer is expected to UPDATE the embedded region when the canonical source (docs/ticket-model.md) legitimately changes — no script, make target, or documented manual copy step is provided for the remediation workflow.
findings.location: Design decisions section / Acceptance Criteria AC#2-AC#3
scenarios: S1 lands and later the canonical rebar-usage section in docs/ticket-model.md is updated; the drift-guard test now fails; the developer must figure out the correct update procedure (copy which exact lines? run what command?) with no guidance — increasing friction and risk of introducing subtle whitespace/encoding divergence in the manual copy
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Makefile target such as `sync-template` that extracts the canonical region from `docs/ticket-model.md` and overwrites only the delimited block in `templates/AGENTS.md`; reference it in the AC and in a comment inside the template near the BEGIN marker.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py` as an affected consumer of the AC5 docs-index edit, and confirm the relative-link format used in the docs reference resolves correctly under that test's broken-link checker.
evidence: tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py: test_current_repo_index_is_clean() calls chk.main(['--check']) against the real docs/README.md
tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py: find_broken_links() resolves relative links within docs/ directory
Plan does not mention test_check_docs_index.py anywhere
findings.finding: The plan's AC5 requires adding a docs-index reference to `templates/AGENTS.md`, but does not enumerate `tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py` as a consumer. That test's `test_current_repo_index_is_clean()` runs against the live `docs/README.md` and `find_broken_links()` validates every relative link in `docs/`. If the docs-index reference is a relative markdown link to `../templates/AGENTS.md` added to any `docs/*.md` file, this test exercises it on every `make test` run; if the templates/ file is missing or the relative path resolves incorrectly, the test will fail.
findings.location: AC5: 'The docs index references templates/AGENTS.md' / Proving: 'rg -n templates/AGENTS.md docs/'
scenarios: The docs-index editor adds a link like `[AGENTS.md template](../templates/AGENTS.md)` in docs/README.md; find_broken_links resolves it against the docs/ tree and may not follow the ../ correctly depending on implementation.
The templates/ directory or AGENTS.md file is missing at test time — test_current_repo_index_is_clean fails unexpectedly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the drift-guard test guards the canonical source's existence with an explicit, human-readable assertion (e.g. `assert CANONICAL.is_file(), 'canonical source docs/ticket-model.md missing — has S1 landed?'`) rather than relying on an unguarded file read that produces a cryptic FileNotFoundError.
evidence: Plan states: 'Depends on S1 (already linked depends_on): the canonical portable rebar-usage content lands in docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop) first.'
docs/ticket-model.md does not exist in the repo (search confirms no match).
AC3 proving command: 'make test' — an unguarded FileNotFoundError on a missing canonical source fails make test with a confusing traceback, not a clear sequencing diagnostic.

test_skill_shared_sync.py uses: 'assert CANONICAL.is_file(), f"canonical shared standard missing: {CANONICAL}"' — a guarded check that produces a clear message; the plan does not specify the analogous guard for the canonical source.
findings.finding: The drift-guard test has an unspecified failure mode when the canonical source file (docs/ticket-model.md, supplied by S1) does not yet exist. If the test does a bare file read without an existence check, it raises an unguarded FileNotFoundError rather than a clear diagnostic, which both breaks CI confusingly and undermines the 'runs under make test' AC. The plan states the S1 dependency but does not specify how the test behaves before S1 lands.
findings.location: Design decisions section / AC3 ('runs under make test')
scenarios: This ticket merges to a branch before S1 is complete, and CI runs make test: the drift-guard test crashes with FileNotFoundError rather than a clear 'canonical source not yet available' assertion.
A developer runs make test locally after checking out this change without S1's docs/ticket-model.md, gets a cryptic failure that doesn't point to the dependency.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to AC3 or the Design decisions section: 'The test must assert canonical source exists with a clear message (e.g., "canonical source docs/ticket-model.md not found — S1 must land first") before attempting the byte-identical comparison, mirroring the test_canonical_file_exists pattern in test_skill_shared_sync.py.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sequencing guard to the drift-guard test (e.g., `pytest.skip` with a clear message when the canonical source file is absent, or a `pytest.importorskip`-style conditional) so that `make test` does not hard-fail when S1 has not yet merged.

findings.finding: The drift-guard test's canonical source file (`docs/ticket-model.md`) does not exist in the repository, and the S1 dependency ticket is unresolvable, meaning the test will be un-runnable (or will fail with a missing-file error) under `make test` until S1 ships — yet the plan has no guard for this sequencing gap.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Depends on S1 (already linked depends_on): the canonical portable rebar-usage content lands in docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop) first.' / AC3: 'A drift-guard test … runs under make test.'
scenarios: A developer pulls this branch before S1 merges and runs `make test` — the new drift-guard test fails with FileNotFoundError on the canonical source, blocking the entire test suite.
CI runs on this branch in parallel with S1 — the new test fails if S1 has not yet merged, creating a merge-order dependency with no gating guard.
The executor ships this plan and marks it done, but S1 slips — the drift-guard test is broken in main for an indeterminate period.
findings.suggested_fix: In `tests/unit/test_client_template_sync.py`, add a module-level guard: `if not CANONICAL.is_file(): pytest.skip('canonical source docs/ticket-model.md absent — S1 not yet merged')`. This makes the test self-documenting about its dependency and prevents it from breaking the test suite while S1 is in flight.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the canonical source to a single, named file path (e.g., `docs/ticket-model.md` alone, or a dedicated `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` that consolidates ticket-model content + the workflow loop) so the test's `CANONICAL` constant is unambiguous.
evidence: Plan text: 'Depends on S1 (already linked depends_on): the canonical portable rebar-usage content lands in docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop) first.'
S1 ticket (073d-51d8-3e58-4b14) only creates docs/ticket-model.md — it never mentions a 'workflow loop' file.
docs/ticket-model.md does not exist in the repo at HEAD (confirmed: search for 'ticket-model' in docs/ returns no file hits).
test_skill_shared_sync.py shows the pattern requires a single CANONICAL path constant — the plan's 'one source' promise is contradicted by the ambiguous '+ the workflow loop' clause.
AC2 proving command only checks for the presence of the BEGIN marker, not which file it is compared against.
findings.finding: The canonical source file for the drift-guard test's CANONICAL constant is never definitively resolved to a single file path: the plan says 'docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop)' but never names what 'the workflow loop' resolves to as a file, and the dependency ticket (073d, S1) only creates docs/ticket-model.md with no 'workflow loop' file. An executor cannot hardcode CANONICAL without this decision.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the canonical portable rebar-usage content lands in docs/ticket-model.md (+ the workflow loop) first'; Design decisions: 'Keep the canonical portable rebar-usage prose in one source'
scenarios: Executor picks only docs/ticket-model.md as CANONICAL, silently omitting the workflow-loop content that was supposed to be in the delimited block.
Executor creates a second file for the workflow loop and concatenates them, producing a test that is incompatible with AC2's single-source claim.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '+ the workflow loop' with either: (a) a pointer to a specific section within docs/ticket-model.md that will contain the workflow loop, or (b) an explicit decision to use a single `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` file that S1 also creates, containing both the ticket model and the workflow loop as the one canonical source.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify (in Design decisions or AC3) what re-sync command the test's module docstring and/or failure message must embed — e.g., a cp/sed one-liner that copies the canonical source content into the delimited region of templates/AGENTS.md.
evidence: test_skill_shared_sync.py lines 8-15: 'sync with: for s in rebar-debug rebar-implement rebar-brainstorm; do cp examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md examples/claude-skills/$s/test-design.md; done' — this instruction-locality pattern is the explicit model the plan references.
The plan says 'mirroring _identical + a divergence self-check' but names only the comparison and self-check mechanics, not the re-sync instruction pattern.
No AC or design decision mentions embedding a re-sync command in the test docstring or failure message.
findings.finding: The plan omits the re-sync instruction that must live inside the test's module docstring (or failure message). The existing test_skill_shared_sync.py explicitly embeds a cp one-liner so a developer reading a red failure knows how to fix it; the plan never specifies the equivalent instruction for test_client_template_sync.py, leaving the test's failure message actionably empty.
findings.location: Design decisions section: 'mirroring _identical + a divergence self-check'; AC3 proving method
scenarios: S1 author updates docs/ticket-model.md; the drift gate goes red; the on-call engineer reads the test failure but finds no command to re-sync the template's delimited block, and must spelunk the plan to figure out the update workflow.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC2's proving step to actually demonstrate byte-identity (e.g., run the drift-guard test in isolation, or use a shell snippet that extracts the delimited block and diffs it against the canonical source file), so the verify step matches what the criterion text claims.
evidence: AC2 text: 'The portable rebar-usage content is a delimited region byte-identical to a single canonical source.'
AC2 proving step: `rg -n 'BEGIN rebar-usage' templates/AGENTS.md` — this confirms only that the BEGIN marker exists in the template; it does not verify byte-identity to any canonical source file.
The design section says the test asserts byte-identity, but the AC's own proof command doesn't exercise that claim.
findings.finding: AC2's verify step only checks for the delimiter's presence, not byte-identity to the canonical source, which is weaker than what the criterion's text claims.
scenarios: An implementer writes a test that checks byte-identity but the acceptance gate (the proving command) passes even if the content inside the delimiters has drifted — the AC would be signed off as done while the actual invariant is unverified at review time.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the proving command with something like: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_client_template_sync.py -v` or a shell one-liner such as `diff <(sed -n '/BEGIN rebar-usage/,/END rebar-usage/p' templates/AGENTS.md) <canonical-source-path>` so the proof command actually exercises byte-identity.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
